task_completion.description: Verify the 'Mitosis' 5D hyperstack sample is opened in ImageJ/Fiji.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ/Fiji window displaying the 'Mitosis' image. The window title should indicate it is the Mitosis sample, and sliders for Z, Channel (c), and Time (t) should be visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the Red channel (chromosomes) is selected for processing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The channel slider at the bottom of the image window is adjusted to display the red channel, visually showing the fluorescent chromosomes.

task_completion.description: Verify a Max Intensity Z-projection is performed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'ZProject' dialog box is visible with 'Max Intensity' selected, OR a new image window (often titled 'MAX_Mitosis') is created. The resulting image must retain the time slider (t) with 51 frames but no longer have a Z-slider.

task_completion.description: Verify the intermediate projection is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog in ImageJ or a file explorer/terminal window showing 'mitosis_projection.tif' being saved to or existing in the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify a line is drawn through the center of the dividing cell.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line selection (usually yellow) is visible on the projection image, crossing directly through both sets of separating chromosomes along the axis of cell division.

task_completion.description: Verify the kymograph is generated by reslicing along the drawn line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Reslice' dialog is used, and a new image window appears showing the kymograph. The image should be a space-time plot showing the chromosomes moving apart over time (often looking like a sideways 'V' or diverging bright lines), with a height corresponding to the 51 time frames.

task_completion.description: Verify the final kymograph is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog in ImageJ or a file explorer/terminal window showing 'mitosis_kymograph.tif' being saved to or existing in the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent uses the ImageJ/Fiji GUI for image processing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with ImageJ menus (e.g., 'Image > Stacks > Z Project', 'Image > Stacks > Reslice') rather than bypassing the UI by running a pre-written macro or Python script from the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the kymograph is genuinely generated from the sample.
integrity.visual_evidence: The kymograph visually matches the expected space-time plot of dividing chromosomes, corresponding exactly to the line drawn on the Mitosis sample, rather than being a downloaded or unrelated image.

integrity.description: Verify files are saved via the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Files are saved using ImageJ's 'Save As' dialogs, not created as empty or dummy files using terminal commands like 'touch' or 'cp'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Mitosis' sample is a 5D hyperstack with 5 Z-slices, 2 channels, and 51 time frames. The intermediate Z-projection must project across the 5 Z-slices while preserving all 51 time frames. The final kymograph is generated by reslicing this 51-frame time-lapse stack along the temporal axis, resulting in an image with a height of exactly 51 pixels. The expected output paths are /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/mitosis_projection.tif and /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/mitosis_kymograph.tif.
